question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildCallServiceParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a TransactionIdParameter with a valid payerAccountId and validStart that matches a historical Hashgraph transaction, causing OpcodeServiceImpl to replay that transaction's EVM execution and expose internal contract state at that historical point without any authorization check?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildCallServiceParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that OpcodeServiceImpl.buildCallServiceParameters() falls back to BlockType.LATEST when recordFileService.findByTimestamp() returns empty, causing a historical transaction replay to execute against the current Hashgraph state instead of the correct historical state, producing a falsified opcode trace?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: processOpcodeCall()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger ContractDebugService.processOpcodeCall() with a consensusTimestamp that sets ctx.setTimestamp(Optional.of(params.getConsensusTimestamp() - 1)), causing the historical state lookup to be off by one nanosecond and returning a different Hashgraph state than the actual state at the time of the original transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: loadWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated requests that cause SystemFileLoader.loadWithRetry() to exhaust its 9 retry attempts on a corrupted historical file entry, causing it to fall back to the genesisFile() and serve genesis-era Hashgraph system file data (fee schedules, exchange rates) for a historical block that should have different values?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/Web3Application.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getReadableStates()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger concurrent requests that cause MirrorNodeState.getReadableStates() to compute and cache a ReadableStates object for a service while another thread is modifying the underlying states map, resulting in a race condition that serves inconsistent historical Hashgraph state to EVM execution?
